subject: First Year Topology
instructions: Answer problems 1–5 and show all your work. Support all statements to the best of your ability. Answer problems 6–15 with complete definitions or statements or short proofs.

problems.text: Show that for every set \(X\), there is no function \(f:X\to 2^X\) such that \(f\) is onto.
Suppose that \(f:X\to Y\) is continuous and that \(A\subset X\) is connected. Show that \(f(A)\subset Y\) is connected.
Let \(f,g:X\to Y\) be continuous maps to a Hausdorff space. Prove that the set \(\{x\mid f(x)=g(x)\}\) is a closed subset of \(X\).
Prove that if \(X\) is a compact Hausdorff space, then \(X\) is a normal space.
State and prove the Contraction Mapping Theorem.
Prove that \(\{(a,b]\mid a<b\}\) is a basis for a topology on \(\mathbb{R}\).
Give the definition of a quotient map.
Show that there is no continuous function \(f:[0,1]\to[0,1)\) which is onto.
Does a connected space need to be path connected?
Show that if \(A\subset\mathbb{R}\) is connected then \(A\) is an interval.
Is \(\mathbb{R}^{\omega}\) connected in the uniform topology?
